user_query:
I need a rigorous TAM/SAM/SOM market sizing for our product, using both bottom-up and top-down methods with clear calculations, ranges, assumptions, and sources. Please read the following files under input/ to ground your work:
- input/product_brief.md (product description, buyer, model, and value prop)
- input/competitor_pricing.csv (competitor names and monthly pricing for ACV estimation)
- input/geo_targets.json (geography and any focus regions)
- input/comparables.md (comparable companies and any revenue/penetration signals)

The product is an AI-powered food safety compliance logging tool for independent restaurants in the United States (HACCP logs, temperature checks, automated reminders, and reporting). Deliverables:
1) Write a structured markdown report to output/market_sizing.md following this exact structure:
- H1 title starting with "# Market Sizing: [Product Name]" and a one-line subtitle with model and geography
- Method 1: Bottom-up section including TAM, SAM, SOM subsections
  - TAM: show explicit math (e.g., N firms × $ACV = $TAM) in a fenced code block, present a single headline number and a conservative range, cite sources inline and in a Sources section
  - SAM: list each filter with % reduction and source; show blended ACV if multiple segments; present a single headline number and a range
  - SOM: show Year 1 and Year 3 targets with penetration rates, customer counts, and 2–3 comparable company validations
- Method 2: Top-down section including TAM, SAM, SOM subsections with the same expectations (headline figures, ranges, math and funnel, sources)
- A "## Sources" section with a markdown table enumerating every key figure and its source
- A "## Summary" section with a table comparing TAM/SAM/SOM for bottom-up vs. top-down side by side, and a short divergence note if they differ by >2×
- A "Key assumptions to validate" section listing at least 3 of the most sensitive assumptions

2) Also write a machine-readable summary to output/summary.json with this schema and units in USD:
{
  "unit": "USD",
  "bottom_up": {
    "TAM": { "point": number, "low": number, "high": number },
    "SAM": { "point": number, "low": number, "high": number },
    "SOM": { "year1": number, "year3": number }
  },
  "top_down": {
    "TAM": { "point": number, "low": number, "high": number },
    "SAM": { "point": number, "low": number, "high": number },
    "SOM": { "year1": number, "year3": number }
  }
}

Important requirements:
- Classify the product by business model and geography upfront and state the revenue unit in one sentence (e.g., "The revenue unit is [X] sold to [Y] at [Z]/year"). Use the input files to estimate ACV via value-created logic and competitor pricing; show your derivation.
- Use NAICS/Census firm counts (or equivalent) for bottom-up TAM; apply appropriate filters for SAM (independent vs. chain, tech readiness, willingness to pay, etc.) with sources.
- Use at least two reputable market research sources for top-down cross-checks; reconcile divergences.
- Provide ranges (not just single numbers) for TAM and SAM, include a blended ACV if multiple segments are relevant, and benchmark SOM penetration rates with at least two comparable companies from input/comparables.md or other reputable public signals.
- Write all outputs only to output/ paths specified above.

If anything is ambiguous in the inputs, document your assumptions explicitly in the report and proceed with conservative estimates.
